disclosure_items.clinical_fact: About an hour ago a jagged, flickering, partly blind patch appeared in the right side of the visual field, lasted a short time, then faded
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe exactly what happened to your vision?
Did the vision change come on suddenly or build up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There was a jagged flickering patch on the right side of my sight, almost like a blind spot with zig-zag edges. It lasted a little while and then it went away.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pins-and-needles that started in the right hand and crept up the arm around the same time, now resolved
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice any numbness, weakness or pins-and-needles anywhere?
How did the numbness spread and how long did it last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My right hand went numb and tingly and it slowly crept up my arm, but that's settled down now too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The visual and sensory symptoms came on gradually and spread (marched) over minutes rather than all at once, and were followed by the headache
trigger_question_examples: Did the symptoms come on all at once, or did they build up and spread over a few minutes?
What happened first — the vision, the numbness or the headache?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It built up over a few minutes and kind of spread — the vision first, then the numbness, and the headache came after that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe one-sided throbbing headache with sensitivity to light, present now
trigger_question_examples: Where is the headache and what does it feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a bad throbbing headache on one side and the light really hurts my eyes, so I'm lying in the dark.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Takes a daily combined (oestrogen-containing) contraceptive pill
trigger_question_examples: Is the pill you take a combined one or a progesterone-only one?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take the combined pill every day — the one with the two hormones in it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no neck stiffness and no skin rash
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any fever, a stiff neck, or a rash?
Does bending your neck forward hurt?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, my neck feels fine and moves normally, and I don't have any rash.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This is the worst-feeling headache the patient recalls but it built up rather than exploding instantly; no vomiting so far
trigger_question_examples: Did the headache reach its worst instantly, like a thunderclap, or build up?
Have you been vomiting or unable to keep fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It built up over a while rather than hitting me all at once, and I haven't been sick, just really sore and sensitive to light.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very anxious about being left without reliable contraception
trigger_question_examples: Is anything else worrying you about today?
How are you feeling about your contraception?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really worried about being unprotected — I don't want to fall pregnant if I have to stop the pill.
